description: Per-school evidence log for British Schools Asia ranking methodology v2. Each school records source-type checks, social-media penalty band classification, press penalty status, host-country freedom (country-level), and four positive signals (inspection, BSO, A Level published, IGCSE published).

sources.note: User access required
Quora has Shanghai schools threads but no school-specific negative pattern
ISR + Expat.com Shanghai forum + SmartShanghai searched
No tier-1 press negative coverage in 36-month window
GoodSchoolsGuide + WhichSchoolAdvisor + ischooladvisor + edarabia + international-schools-database — assessed

v2.socialMediaBandReason: 2025 IB average 38.1; 20th anniversary year. GoodSchoolsGuide notes a recent bullying case handled by school but is a single source. No 3-source negative pattern.
v2.pressNegativeReason: No tier-1 outlet (FT/Reuters/Bloomberg/SCMP/Straits Times/Bangkok Post/Nikkei Asia) coverage of school-specific issues in last 18 months. Historical coverage (2016-2019 SCMP coverage of Harrow HK, NAIS HK, GSIS) is outside the v2 18-month window.
v2.aLevelRef: A Level not in curricula
v2.igcseRef: Curricula declares IGCSE; first cohort completed; standard for premium schools to publish.

yew-chung-international-school-hong-kong.name: Yew Chung International School of Hong Kong

sources.note: User access required
GeoExpat thread on YCIS feedback — mixed
edu-kingdom forum discussion — mixed-to-negative
No tier-1 press in last 36 months

ISR forum thread (multiple posts to Feb 2026): bullying through racism toward students AND staff, Instagram gossip account, "fostering hate between students and teachers", high fees + over-commercialization, poor management, contract non-renewals without explanation
Indeed staff reviews — recurring management/turnover concerns
ischooladvisor — mixed reviews; some positive on kindergarten and IB results, some negative on culture and management
WhichSchoolAdvisor — generally positive on bilingual programme

v2.socialMediaBandReason: Documented across 4 independent non-school sources within 36 months (ISR, Indeed, edu-kingdom forum, ischooladvisor): recurring concerns on management, staff turnover, commercialization, learning environment, and reports of bullying/racism toward both students and staff. Pattern fits "repeated moderate criticism" per methodology, not a major single scandal.
v2.pressNegativeReason: No tier-1 outlet (FT/Reuters/Bloomberg/SCMP/Straits Times/Bangkok Post/Nikkei Asia) coverage of school-specific issues in last 18 months. Historical coverage (2016-2019 SCMP coverage of Harrow HK, NAIS HK, GSIS) is outside the v2 18-month window.
v2.aLevelRef: A Level not in curricula
v2.igcseRef: Curricula declares IGCSE; first cohort completed; standard for premium schools to publish.

sources.note: User access required

ischooladvisor — multiple parent 2-star reviews citing fundamental lack of academic support, ambiguous progress reporting, "basic maths and English far below sub-standard"; one parent describes as "year-long summer camp"
Glassdoor staff reviews — toxic management in secondary, "unhealthy paranoid culture", senior leadership "universally disliked and socially deficient"
ISR forum thread referenced; could not verify content depth

v2.socialMediaBandReason: 3 independent non-school sources within 36 months document recurring moderate concerns: ischooladvisor (parent academic concerns and weak progress reporting), Glassdoor (toxic management and paranoid culture in secondary), GeoExpat (community discussion noting concerns). Pattern meets 10% moderate threshold; not a major single scandal so 25% not warranted.
